sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I'm way over the action budget. Let me think more carefully about the puzzle before trying another approach.
Current placement (column-interleaved): O, R, Y in red; p, b, N, M in green - didn't work.
Let me look at the top reference row again: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M
And the bottom palette: R, p, N, O, M, b, Y
I've tried:
1. Top row order left-to-right: O, p, R | b, N, Y, M - NO
2. Bottom palette order: R, p, N | O, M, b, Y - NO
3. Column-interleaved: O, R, Y | p, b, N, M - NO
Let me think about what other patterns could work. The top row has 7 colors. The red area has 3 slots and green has 4 slots.
What if the grouping is based on the border color? The red border area should contain colors "related" to red, and green border area should contain colors "related" to green.
Looking at the top row: O(orange), p(purple), R(red), b(blue), N(light green), Y(yellow), M(magenta/pink)
Red-related: R, O, M (red, orange, magenta/pink)
Green-related: N, Y, b, p (light green, yellow, blue, purple)
So red area: R, O, M and green area: N, Y, b, p
But in what order? Maybe the order within each group follows the top row order?
Top row: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M
Red colors in top row order: O, R, M
Green colors in top row order: p, b, N, Y
So: red area = O, R, M; green area = p, b, N, Y
Let me try this!

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# New hypothesis: group by border color affinity
# Red area: red-related colors from top row order = O, R, M
# Green area: green-related colors from top row order = p, b, N, Y
# Clear all
for _ in range(7):
    action(['ACTION7'])

# Place: red=O,R,M; green=p,b,N,Y
actions = []
# red-22 = O
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 31})
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 22, 'col': 22})
# red-28 = R
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 10})
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 22, 'col': 28})
# red-40 = M
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 38})
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 22, 'col': 40})
# green-22 = p
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 17})
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 22})
# green-28 = b
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 45})
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 28})
# green-34 = N
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 24})
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 34})
# green-40 = Y
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 58, 'col': 52})
actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 36, 'col': 40})
actions.append('SPACE')

action(actions)
print(f"Result: level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
